experiment: DOMEX-CAT-S452 — FMEA refresh S445→S452 + 2 hardening fixes + NAT window
method: FMEA audit: searched L-1093 through L-1102 for failure patterns. Checked tools for N=1000 breakpoints. Investigated cascade A→K vs cascade_monitor discrepancy. Fixed 2 bugs directly.
expect: FMEA refresh S452: 0-1 new FMs within NAT S450-S465 window. Cascade A→K maps to specific FMs.
actual: 7 candidate FMs found (NAT prediction CONFIRMED — within S450-S465 window, well beyond 0-1 expected). 4 registered (FM-31 through FM-34). 2 fixed in-session: FM-31 (dispatch_scoring.py first_frontier searched full file instead of active_section — showed CONFIRMED frontiers as recommendations), FM-32 (cascade_monitor.py S435 stale fallback — self-referential reliability failure). A→K cascade was stale HEALTH.md cache, not active cascade — cascade_monitor shows 0 cascades. NAT class prediction: scale-bound monitoring failures at N=1000 (FM-33, FM-34).
diff: Expected 0-1 new FMs, found 7 candidates (4 registered). NAT timing CONFIRMED but magnitude exceeded (7 vs 0-1). Class: scale-bound monitoring + dispatch-frontier scope — matches historical migration pattern (infrastructure→system-design→concurrency→epistemology→scale-monitoring). Two fixes applied in-session (FM-31, FM-32).

new_fms.name: Dispatch top_frontier scope overflow — shows resolved/confirmed frontiers
new_fms.evidence: dispatch_scoring.py line 116 searched full content instead of active_section. F-EXP12 (CONFIRMED) still appeared as dispatch recommendation.
new_fms.fix: Changed regex target from content to active_section in dispatch_scoring.py

new_fms.name: cascade_monitor.py stale fallback — self-referential reliability failure
new_fms.evidence: _current_session() fallback was hardcoded S435 (17 sessions stale). Self-excluded from its own stale-baseline scan. When git log fails, produces wrong stale counts.
new_fms.fix: Changed fallback to derive from lesson count (glob memory/lessons/L-*.md)

new_fms.name: N=1000 enforcement-dilution waypoint unstructured (L-601 instance)
new_fms.evidence: maintenance_health.py check_scale_waypoints() fires DUE at N>=1000 but cadence change is advisory only. At N>=3 concurrent sessions, DUE compliance is near 0% (L-526).
new_fms.fix_path: Wire automatic cadence update into maintenance_health.py or post-commit hook

new_fms.name: Retention-accessibility divergence at N=1000+ (L-1096 prescription unimplemented)
new_fms.evidence: L-1096: storage metrics 94-100% mask access metrics 0-31%. No check_retention_accessibility() in maintenance_health.py. INDEX.md pointer coverage 13.4% at N=986. No automated alert for access degradation.
new_fms.fix_path: Add check_retention_accessibility() to maintenance_health.py N>=1000 waypoint

cascade_investigation.explanation: HEALTH.md stale cache from S445. K layer FAILING (BLIND-SPOT 15.3%, DECAYED 28.7%) but not cascading with A layer. A→K not in ADJACENCY map (A adjacent to T, Q only).
cascade_investigation.action: HEALTH.md cascade status is stale — needs refresh

verdict: NAT timing CONFIRMED, magnitude EXCEEDED. 4 new FMs registered (30→34). 2 fixed in-session. Scale-monitoring layer is the new failure surface at N=1000+.
